---
name: Document
description: Describe when to use this prompt
---

<!-- Tip: Use /create-prompt in chat to generate content with agent assistance -->

We are going to ensure that the readme fully reflects only the current state of the gui_do public API. Follows this format: gui_do as a project name, and then at most a few-sentence highest-level overview of what the package provides to a developer audience.  After that section will be a table of contents that markdown links to every section and sub-section and for each link to an item the displayed text for the link is either the item's section or sub-section name. In each section and sub-section there is another link to the right of the section or sub-section name labelled "Back to Top" which links back to the top of the table of contents. Format the table of contents in the clearest way markdown format allows. The next section is a quick start section, followed by an overview section written in plain English of what the major systems are in the package, how they work with each other, and what kind of applications the faculties they provide are best suited for.  This overview should be fairly comprehensive and must include every important point at the least and then what helps the developer reading the text understand how gui_do should be used. The next section should be called "Minimal Runnable Example" and have only a code listing which is what the title describes. Follow that with a "Package Management" section with "Start a New Project" and "Add to or Update an Existing Project" sub-sections that detail the usage of the scripts/manage.py tool. For subsequent sections, update the readme by analyzing the complete gui package public API only to determine current state, organize the order of sections from the current state so that like concepts and sections are together and for sub-sections similarly organize in order of like concepts. From the current state update all relevant sections and sub-sections to include information and tutorials for all missing information. The overall order of these subsequent sections should be in conceptual order for using the package. Remove any information that is not part of the public API. Ensure all code listings match the current API.
